questions.text: What is a Virtual BOM (VBOM) in the Order Management process?
options: A template for mass upload of sales orders.
A specific material code used to order a group of materials together on a sales order.
A discount mechanism for promotional sales.
A storage configuration for warehouse operations.

questions.text: Which Virtual BOM type allows for independent quantity adjustments of its components in a sales order?
options: ZUMF
ZVIR
ZUMC
ZVIA

questions.text: What happens when an order is created without a Purchase Order (PO) Type?
options: The order is automatically approved.
The system assigns a default PO Type.
The order falls into incompletion.
The order is rejected.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about ATP (Available to Promise) confirmation for a ZUMF Virtual BOM?
options: Partial ATP confirmations allow the order to proceed.
All components must have confirmed ATP for delivery.
ATP confirmation is not required for ZUMF BOMs.
Delivery can proceed even if some components have zero stock.

questions.text: How are pricing conditions applied to Virtual BOMs during a sales order?
options: Pricing is applied only to the VBOM header.
Pricing is calculated at the component level of the VBOM.
Pricing is determined by the total weight of the VBOM.
Pricing is the same for all VBOMs regardless of type.
